description_long: This object consists of three cylindrical canisters with metallic caps, bound together by tan straps and connected by visible black and red wiring. Attached to the side is a black rectangular electronic module featuring an antenna and a small red light, suggesting remote or timed activation. The canisters are arranged in parallel, with the wiring looping around and connecting to the electronic module, which appears to serve as the control or detonation unit. The overall construction is compact and utilitarian, with exposed wires and components indicating a makeshift or improvised design. The materials appear to be a combination of metal, plastic, and rubber, with a glossy black finish on the canisters and matte metallic ends. The object is visually striking due to its industrial appearance and the presence of electronic and explosive elements.
description: A compact, improvised bomb with three black cylindrical canisters, metallic caps, tan straps, and an attached electronic module with visible wiring and an antenna.
description_view_0: Front view shows the electronic module with antenna and red light, as well as the wiring and straps securing the three canisters.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the cylindrical arrangement of the canisters and the looping wires, with the electronic module partially visible.
description_view_2: Rear angled view reveals the metallic caps of the canisters and the continuation of the wiring, with less visibility of the electronic module.
description_view_3: Opposite side view shows the canisters' alignment, the tan straps, and the electronic module with antenna from a different angle.
attribute_detonation_mechanism: Detonation appears to be controlled by an electronic module with an antenna, likely for remote activation, and visible wiring that may connect to a triggering circuit.
